title: Jungić et al. Conjecture on $T_k$
context_text: Let $T_k$ denote the smallest positive integer $t$ such that for every positive integer $m$, every equinumerous $t$-coloring of $[tm]$ contains a rainbow $k$-term arithmetic progression. Jungi\'{c} et al.\ proved $c'k^2 \leq T_k \leq ck^3$ and conjectured that the lower bound gives the correct order of magnitude; this conjecture was reiterated in a subsequent survey [47]. The lower bound $T_k \geq c'k^2$ was later sharpened by Fox, Jungi\'{c}, and Radoi\v{c}i\'{c} [34].
paper_contribution: Proves $T_k \leq ck^2 \log k$ via Corollary 2.4, obtained by combining Corollary 2.3 on sub-Ramsey numbers with the main independent arithmetic progression result (Theorem 2.2); this improves the previous upper bound $T_k \leq ck^3$ and reduces the gap to a single logarithm.
notes: PDF source. The full resolution $T_k = \Theta(k^2)$ remains open after this paper.
paper_title: Short proofs of some extremal results III
